arts.ttl: Aston Villa team news vs West Ham confirmed as Rogers, Maatsen and Olsen start
arts.sum: Villa starting XI: Olsen, Cash, Konsa, Mings, Maatsen, Kamara, Barkley, Bailey, Tielemans, Rogers, Watkins. Unai Emery has named his Aston Villa team to take on West Ham in the FA Cup third round.

arts.ttl: Why Emi Martinez is missing from Aston Villa squad to face West Ham in FA Cup
arts.sum: Aston Villa have released a special commemorative kit to celebrate the club's 150th anniversary - and you can get your hands on one now. Gauci started both of Villa's Carabao Cup games against Wycombe Wanderers and Crystal Palace, but Olsen was injured at the time.

arts.ttl: FA Cup third round LIVE! Potter names first West Ham line-up
arts.sum: Unai Emery has backed Graham Potter to succeed at West Ham ahead of their meeting with his Aston Villa side in the FA Cup . Speaking ahead of Friday's clash at Villa Park, Emery was asked about facing the new West Ham boss in his first game in charge of the Hammers.

Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. West Ham United - Fri, Jan 10th 2025, 20:00 Tyrone Mings captained Aston Villa for their FA Cup third-round tie against West Ham United at Villa Park. The centre-back wore the armband in the absence of John McGinn and Emi Martínez, as Unai Emery made three changes from last weekend’s Premier League victory over Leicester City. Robin Olsen, Ian Maatsen and Morgan Rogers were named in Villa’s starting XI, with the latter returning from a one-match suspension. Martínez was not named in the matchday 20 to face the Hammers, with McGinn injured, while Lucas Digne was included among the substitutes. He was joined by Academy graduate Bradley Burrowes, who took his place in Villa’s senior squad for the first time. The eagerly-anticipated evening saw the club continue its season-long celebrations of our 150th anniversary.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa v West Ham United: FA Cup third round – live
arts.sum: FA Cup updates from the 8pm GMT kick-off at Villa Park Live scoreboard | Read Football Daily | And mail Scott | Graham Potter is celebrating his 24-hour anniversary as West Ham United boss, and here’s the new boss talking to ITV. “West Ham are a fantastic football club … great history, tradition … ambition … a family feel … a well-functioning academy … an ambitious board … it felt really right … I feel like I've come back stronger … a better person and coach … I’m really excited … we had a training session yesterday … met everybody … tried to get my bearings … prepared the team … a day in the hotel today as well … the response has been brilliant … the players have taken on little messages … we understand what we’re trying to do so onto the game.” Aston Villa are celebrating their 150th anniversary tonight. To mark the occasion, various match-day items have been priced at £1.50, including lager, pies, sausage rolls, soft drinks, scarves, beanie hats, caps, the commemorative programme, and the shuttle bus from Birmingham city centre to Villa Park. That’s a lovely touch. The commemorative shirt isn’t priced at £1.50, mind, but let’s not expect the moon on a stick. In any case, that’s not stopped the kit already selling out online, and you can understand why, because it’s delicious: all black, reflecting the club’s first colours before they transitioned to their storied claret and blue, with retro rampant-lion crest, no sponsors, and no names on the back. Happy birthday, Villa! Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Potter selects teenager Scarles in first West Ham team
arts.sum: West Ham United: Fabiański, Wan-Bissaka, Mavropanos, Kilman, Scarles, Álvarez, Souček ©, Paquetá, Kudus, Summerville, Füllkrug The substitutes’ bench will be comprised of goalkeeper Wes Foderingham, defenders Vladimír Coufal, Aaron Cresswell and Scarles’ fellow FA Youth Cup winner Kaelan Casey, midfielders Guido Rodríguez, Andy Irving, Carlos Soler and Luis Guilherme, and striker Danny Ings.

arts.ttl: Is bringing Moyes back the right move for Everton?
arts.sum: They say in football there is no room for sentiment.

arts.ttl: Karren Brady contradicts Sullivan 'whispers' as she speaks out after West Ham hire Potter
arts.sum: Karren Brady has contradicted reports that David Sullivan didn’t want to appoint Graham Potter as West Ham manager. The Englishman was appointed as head coach... The post Karren Brady contradicts Sullivan 'whispers' as she speaks out after West Ham hire Potter appeared first on West Ham Zone.

arts.ttl: Everton hopeful of swift David Moyes appointment after productive talks
arts.sum: Former manager to return with two-and-a-half year deal Owners hope Moyes can steer team away from relegation Everton are hopeful of appointing David Moyes as Sean Dyche’s replacement after a day of productive talks with the club’s former manager. Moyes met representatives from The Friedkin Group (TFG), Everton’s new owner, on Friday to discuss a return to the club he managed between 2002 and 2013. Negotiations are understood to have advanced well and there is confidence a deal could be concluded within the next 24 hours. The length of contract is being discussed but the expectation is that but the expectation is that it will be a two-and-a-half year deal. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Kylian Mbappe prepares to send starlet to Aston Villa
arts.sum: Mbappe took over the Normandy outfit in the summer with an eye on developing young talent that could be sold on at a profit and the first major deal to be completed by the club under the stewardship of the Real Madrid star is Tidiam Gomis . Aston Villa are in “advanced talks” to sign a starlet from Kylian Mbappe ’s Ligue 2 club Caen.
